      "Warranty explicitly excludes labor, limiting recourse if brake work fails prematurely",
      "No fluid disposal or brake fluid system assessment mentioned despite full brake system service"

    "summary": "Price is fair to slightly above average for a comprehensive four-wheel brake service on an economy vehicle. The $1,581.85 total breaks down to $940 in parts (premium aftermarket brands Bosch and Brembo specified for front), $540 in labor at a reasonable $135/hr independent shop rate, and $102 in tax and supplies. The 4-hour labor estimate aligns with typical book time for a complete brake pad and rotor replacement. Red flags include unspecified parts quality for rear components and lack of labor warranty coverage, which reduces customer protection. The 12-month parts warranty is standard but limited in scope. Overall, this quote reflects transparent pricing with quality material choices, though labor cost visibility could be improved by itemizing time per brake corner.",

  "summary_for_llm": "Total: $1,581.85.\nVehicle: 2018 Honda Civic (87,000 mi).\nLabor rate: $135/hr.\nLabor hours: 4.\nParts total: $935.\n\n5 repair item(s):\n  - Front brake pads (Bosch QuietCast) [soon]\n  - Front rotors (Brembo) [soon]\n  - Rear brake pads [soon]\n  - Rear rotors [soon]\n  - Labor: 4 hours @ $135/hr [soon]\n\n4 red flag(s):\n  - Rear brake pad and rotor parts type not specified (OEM vs. aftermarket) -- only front components labeled as brand names\n  - Labor breakdown not provided by repair item -- 4 hours quoted as lump sum without detail on time per service\n  - Warranty explicitly excludes labor, limiting recourse if brake work fails prematurely\n  - No fluid disposal or brake fluid system assessment mentioned despite full brake system service\n\nSummary: Price is fair to slightly above average for a comprehensive four-wheel brake service on an economy vehicle. The $1,581.85 total breaks down to $940 in parts (premium aftermarket brands Bosch and Brembo specified for front), $540 in labor at a reasonable $135/hr independent shop rate, and $102 in tax and supplies. The 4-hour labor estimate aligns with typical book time for a complete brake pad and rotor replacement. Red flags include unspecified parts quality for rear components and lack of labor warranty coverage, which reduces customer protection. The 12-month parts warranty is standard but limited in scope. Overall, this quote reflects transparent pricing with quality material choices, though labor cost visibility could be improved by itemizing time per brake corner."
